channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Trump says Iran will 'pay the price' and claims they have 'taken too long' to agree to a deal
item.description: The president's comments came after U.S. forces on Tuesday evening launched strikes against Iran.

item.title: The May inflation numbers are due out Wednesday morning. Here's what to expect
item.description: If the Wall Street consensus is correct, the consumer price index is expected to show inflation running at a 4.2% annual rate.

item.title: Oil prices jump after Trump says Iran will 'pay the price' for failing at peace deal
item.description: Oil prices rose after the U.S. launched military strikes against Iran, raising concerns that renewed clashes may threaten shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: Super Micro Computer, Cracker Barrel, Nike and more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves in the premarket.

item.title: It’s time to get back into this fast-casual stock, UBS says
item.description: The bank thinks shares of Cava are set to rise on the back of same-store sales growth and a growing number of locations.

item.title: Two reasons for optimism after Tuesday's whipsaw market sell-off
item.description: There's reason for optimism, judging by bullish options flows around stocks that do better when interest-rates stay lower, and call-buyers who are prepared for the biggest move in Oracle since Covid.

item.title: How pandemic car shortages are still making new and used cars expensive
item.description: Pandemic-era shortages are still pushing up used car prices, and automakers learned lessons from that time that stands to keep supply tight

item.title: Bill Gates to face questions about Jeffrey Epstein from House Oversight panel
item.description: Gates reportedly prepared for his testimony with the help of Jake Greenberg, who previously was the Oversight committee's chief investigations counsel.

item.title: The World Cup will likely be the biggest gambling event in history
item.description: Global wagers on the 2026 World Cup could top $50 billion, according to Macquarie analyst Chad Beynon. That’s up from more than $35 billion in the 2022 tournament.

item.title: Gold, silver and bitcoin fall as traders up Fed rate hike bets
item.description: Precious metals were firmly in negative territory, as fears about inflation and the Federal Reserve's interest rate path weighed on investor sentiment.

item.title: Weekly mortgage demand surges nearly 11% higher, despite volatile interest rates
item.description: Mortgage demand from both current homeowners and potential buyers rose sharply, even though interest rates climbed as well.

item.title: SpaceX IPO explained: The price is set, but retail allocation still up in the air
item.description: Charles Schwab, Fidelity, Robinhood, SoFi and Morgan Stanley's E-Trade are among the brokerage platforms making SpaceX shares available.

item.title: GM eyes new battery chemistry to grow AI data center, energy storage business
item.description: GM is expanding efforts to capitalize on the expected growth of energy storage and data centers and the development of next-generation sodium-ion batteries.

item.title: Super Micro stock tumbles on $7 billion financing plans as company touts AI server orders
item.description: Super Micro is the latest company tied to the AI boom to announce that it's tapping the capital markets.

item.title: $70 billion immigration funding package passes in U.S. House in a win for Speaker Johnson
item.description: The U.S. House on Tuesday approved a $70 billion immigration enforcement funding package after months of debate.

item.title: Anthropic releases Mythos-like AI model to the public two months after private rollout rocked Wall Street
item.description: Anthropic said the broad release is possible because of new safeguards that block responses in specific high-risk areas.

item.title: Democrat Xavier Becerra and Republican Steve Hilton will face off in California governor’s race
item.description: Republican Steve Hilton has won enough votes to advance to the general election, the Associated Press determined Tuesday. He'll face Democrat Xavier Becerra.

item.title: These dividend stocks have slashed their payments, Morgan Stanley says
item.description: Shares of some companies could turn around if they clean up their act after cutting their dividend payments.

item.title: As LIV Golf faces a Saudi funding cliff, CEO says to take PIF 'at their word'
item.description: PIF is set to stop funding the golf league after the 2026 season, though LIV's Scott O'Neil could not guarantee that the final four tournaments will take place.

item.title: Another jump in Boeing deliveries shows why we got into the stock and want to stay in
item.description: It's key to assessing the aircraft manufacturer's turnaround story under CEO Kelly Ortberg.

item.title: Meta agrees to Indian AI data center deal as hyperscaler bolsters its infrastructure
item.description: Meta signs a deal with Mukesh Ambani's Reliance Industries to lease an AI-enabled data center in India.

item.title: Want more travel rewards? Start with your bank account
item.description: Mileage savings accounts and co-branded airline/hotel debit cards help you earn travel rewards without having to take on credit.

item.title: Beijing escalating AI espionage to catch up with the U.S. on tech, cybersecurity firm says
item.description: U.S. cybersecurity giant CrowdStrike said China-based entities made over half of state-sponsored cyberattacks on tech firms for artificial intelligence assets.

item.title: Entergy CEO pushes back on fears that AI data centers will drive up electricity bills
item.description: Entergy CEO Drew Marsh said data centers can be a win for local communities rather than a burden on residents.

item.title: Kalshi rolls out whistleblower services, employment verification to curb insider trading
item.description: The platform will require traders to disclose their employer's identity when trading on sensitive markets, a Wall Street Journal article first reported.

item.title: What next for BP? Leadership exits test investor confidence in board oversight
item.description: An executive exodus at BP has sparked intense scrutiny from investors.

item.title: SoftBank sinks over 8% as global tech stocks tumble, tracking Wall Street losses
item.description: Semiconductor and technology stocks fell on Wednesday, tracking overnight losses on Wall Street.

item.title: China May wholesale inflation hits near 4-year high on Iran war-led higher input costs, AI boom
item.description: The Middle East conflict has given China's economy a reflationary boost as energy and raw material disruptions deepened.

item.title: Energy prices take center stage as the ECB prepares to decide on rates
item.description: The ECB is expected to raise rates by 25 basis points as higher energy prices drive inflation and raise concerns about broader price pressures.

item.title: Trump family got about $500M from crypto venture — but investors saw steep losses
item.description: Eric Trump and Donald Trump Jr. celebrated a deal with Alt5 Sigma that generated roughly $500 million for the Trump family. The company's shares are down 93%.
